preamble: The shift from the bilabial nasal [m] to the mid-back rounded vowel [o] is a form of vocalization. In syllable coda positions, the labial closure of the [m] can be lost, leaving only the lip-rounding and back resonance, which merges into the vowel space.

examples.note: Historical nasal-consonant sequences merged into nasalized vowels, which in Polish often have an [o]-like quality.
